releases.name: v1.8.12
releases.body:
Version 1.8.12 - Duplicate-on-OS badges, [OS] title links, clearer big-file extraction errors

Three forum-requested improvements from 0pener (forum post p54971):

1) "N on OS" badge per matched subtitle row (per imdb + language)
   - New XmlRpcService.searchSubtitlesByImdb with 24h cache (de-duped by
     imdb+lang key — 20 episodes in 1 language = at most 20 API calls)
   - New useAutoOsDuplicateCount hook that fires when (movie, language)
     settles for each pair; results fanned out to all rows sharing the key
   - MatchedPairs row, right side (ml-auto), surfaces:
       🟡 N on OS   — amber badge linking to OS search filtered by
                       imdbid + sublanguageid
       🟢 0 on OS — new language for this title — positive "fresh"
                       signal in green
       ⏳ checking OS… / ⚠️ OS check failed — transient states
   - Replaces the long-disabled "Language-specific subtitle count" stub

2) [OS] links next to every IMDB ID in the movie display
   - Two new helpers in fileUtils.js: getOsImdbSearchUrl(imdbId) and
     getOsImdbLanguageSearchUrl(imdbId, sublang)
   - Six [OS] links inserted across MovieDisplay (3 link positions × 2
     code branches: featuresData + XML-RPC fallback) — next to Episode,
     TV Series, and Movie/Main IMDB IDs
   - 0pener's complaint was "titles link to IMDB twice, but not to OS";
     now every IMDB ID has a sibling [OS] link

3) Clearer error when extraction fails on huge MKVs
   - Root cause is NOT mkvmerge version (0pener's hypothesis was a
     correlation): FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer hits a ~2GB browser
     memory cap. @opensubtitles/video-metadata-extractor's "88GB"
     chunking reassembles all chunks into one Blob, which still needs
     a single ArrayBuffer when handed to @ffmpeg/util's fetchFile()
   - New BROWSER_FILEREADER_SOFT_LIMIT (2GB) and pre-flight warning
     when file size exceeds it
   - New humanizeExtractorError(error, file) rewrites the cryptic
     "Failed to extract metadata: File could not be read! Code=-1" into:
       Browser cannot read files this large (X.XX GB). The browser's
       FileReader hits a ~2 GB memory cap regardless of the extractor's
       chunking. Workaround: extract the subtitle tracks locally with
       mkvextract or ffmpeg, then drop the .srt files onto the
       uploader directly.
   - Wrapped at all three throw sites in mkvSubtitleExtractor.js
   - Bug-fix bonus: UI's "extraction_failed" status was set but never
     rendered (badge only matched "error"). Combined them — both now
     surface as "❌ Extraction failed" with the humanized error in
     the tooltip
   - TODO comment documents the durable fix: Tauri sidecar spawning
     native mkvextract/ffmpeg (tauri-plugin-shell + filesystem already
     in place; needs binary bundling + signing per-platform)

Tests: 207/207 pass. Build clean.

releases.name: v1.8.11
releases.body:
Version 1.8.11 - Fix Hearing Impaired false positives on title "Hi"

The auto-detection regex `\bhi\b` and `.hi.` were matching any "Hi" in a release
filename, falsely flagging ~500 episodes and ~100 movies as Hearing Impaired.

Forum-reported failing cases (now correctly classified as NOT HI):
  - The.Hi-Lo.Country.1998.1080p.USA.BluRay.AVC.DTS-HD.MA.5.1-rmHD
  - The.King.of.Queens.S03E13.Hi.Def.Jam.1080p.NF.WEB-DL.AAC2.0.H.264-POWER
  - For.All.Mankind.S01E07.Hi.Bob.1080p.WEB-DL.6CH.x265.HEVC-PSA

Fix in src/services/subtitleUploadService.js:
  - checkHearingImpairedFromString: dropped bare \bhi\b / hi[_-] / [_-]hi.
    Now only fires on sdh, psdh, hearing.impaired variants, or a path part
    that IS exactly "hi" (e.g. an /HI/ directory).
  - checkHearingImpairedFromFilename: switched to positional token matching.
    Strip subtitle extension, tokenize on .-_, then look for hi/sdh/psdh/
    hearingimpaired only in the LAST 3 TOKENS — the language-code / feature-
    flag slot where real HI markers live (movie.eng.hi.srt, show.sdh.srt).
    hearing.impaired keyword still matches anywhere.

Real HI markers in the language-code slot still detect correctly:
  - movie.hi.srt, movie.eng.hi.srt, movie-hi-en.srt, show.s01e01.sdh.srt

Tests: 48 pass (38 pre-existing + 10 new regression tests covering the
three forum cases and reverse-direction "must still detect" sanity checks).

releases.name: v1.8.10
releases.body:
🔧 FIX: Bump guessit-js 1.0.1 → ^4.0.0 (v1.0.1 unpublished from npm registry)

CI builds for v1.8.10 failed with:
  npm error 404 Not Found - GET https://registry.npmjs.org/guessit-js/-/guessit-js-1.0.1.tgz

guessit-js@1.0.1 has been unpublished from npm. The package now starts at 3.8.1
with 4.0.0 as latest (same publisher: opensubtitles). Earlier builds worked off
GitHub Actions' cache; fresh `npm ci` can no longer fetch v1.0.1.

API surface in src/services/offlineGuessItService.js (`import { guessit }`,
`guessit(filename)`, `guessit(filename, options)`) is unchanged between 1.x and
4.x. Verified end-to-end locally:
  - npm test → 197/197 pass
  - OfflineGuessItService.guessFromFilename('The.Man.In.The.High.Castle.S01E01.1080p.mkv')
    → {title: 'The Man In The High Castle', season: 1, episode: 1}
  - npm run build → clean vite build

releases.name: v1.8.9
releases.body:
Version 1.8.9 - VIP Lifetime Member Upload Restriction

- Removed 'vip lifetime member' from allowed upload ranks
- Updated rank validation in userService.js
- Updated RankRestrictionWarning.jsx to reflect new permissions
- Users with VIP Lifetime Member rank can no longer use PRO uploader tool

Allowed ranks: super admin, translator, trusted member, administrator,
moderator, gold member, platinum member, trusted, subtranslator, os legend

releases.name: v1.8.8
releases.body:
Version 1.8.8 - Upload Order & Anonymous Features

- HI subtitles now upload before non-HI for same movie/language
- Added 'Upload as Anonymous' feature for OS Legend rank users
- Anonymous login implemented with proper error handling
- Added 'Check for Updates' button in app footer
- Fixed Tauri dev server port configuration

releases.name: v1.8.5
releases.body:
Version 1.8.5 - Validation UX Improvements & Bug Fixes

- Scroll directly to problematic subtitle when clicking validation errors
- Highlight subtitles with validation errors (red border/background)
- Fixed "Login to Upload" button not opening login dialog
- Fixed language filter double-counting orphaned subtitles
- Only show language validation errors after detection completes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v1.8.4 - Build 198
releases.body:
## Version 1.8.4 - Advanced Language Filter with Dynamic Updates

### NEW FEATURES
- **Language Filter** - Filter subtitles by detected language before upload
- **Select/Deselect All** - Quick language selection controls  
- **Dynamic Updates** - Filter updates when languages are manually changed
- **Auto-Remove Empty** - Languages with 0 subtitles automatically removed
- **Real-time Counts** - Subtitle counts update as languages change
- **Smart Auto-Select** - Initial auto-select, respects manual deselections
- **Select Folder Button** - Added directory picker next to file picker

### ENHANCEMENTS
- Fixed Chrome multi-directory drag-drop bug (two-phase processing)
- Improved language detection display (uses language_code field)
- Better spacing in language filter component
- Comprehensive debug logging for language filter logic

### TECHNICAL DETAILS
- LanguageFilter component with checkbox grid layout
- Integration with getSubtitleLanguage for manual/auto detection
- Smart useEffect with previousLanguagesRef tracking
- Prevents re-selecting after user deselects

releases.name: v1.8.1 - Automatic Release Name Cleaning
releases.body:
Version 1.8.1 - Automatic Release Name Cleaning

NEW FEATURE: Smart Release Name Cleaning

This release adds automatic cleaning of release names during subtitle uploads, removing language codes and other markers to ensure clean, professional release names on OpenSubtitles.org.

Key Features:
- Automatically removes language codes (eng, spa, por, fra, etc.)
- Removes regional language variants (pt-PT, pt-BR, en-US, es-ES, etc.)
- Removes full language names (English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, etc.)
- Removes CD markers (CD1, CD I)
- Removes SDH/HI suffixes
- Removes addic7ed.com and other custom patterns
- Maintains quality information (1080p, BluRay, WEB-DL, etc.)

Examples:
- Before: "Prisoner.of.War.2025.1080p.AMZN.WEB-DL.DDP5.1.H.264.v2.pt-PT"
  After:  "Prisoner.of.War.2025.1080p.AMZN.WEB-DL.DDP5.1.H.264.v2"

- Before: "Movie.Name.2024.1080p.BluRay.x264.eng.sdh"
  After:  "Movie.Name.2024.1080p.BluRay.x264"

- Before: "Series.S01E01.CD1.Spanish"
  After:  "Series.S01E01"

Technical Details:
- New utility module: src/utils/releaseNameUtils.js
- Integrated into subtitle upload service for both paired and orphaned subtitles
- Uses combined language data from REST API and XML-RPC
- Comprehensive test coverage: 18 new tests (all passing)
- Debug logging shows before/after comparison

Test Results: 159 tests passing (100% pass rate)
- 18 new tests for release name cleaning
- All existing tests continue to pass
- Full integration with upload workflow

This improvement ensures that release names uploaded to OpenSubtitles.org are clean, professional, and free of language-specific markers that belong in other metadata fields.
